Aditya Birla Sun Life Insurance Assured Savings Plan –
Aditya Birla Sun Life Insurance Assured Savings Plan – Frequently Asked Question
1. What is ABSLI Assured Savings Plan?
ABSLI Assured Savings Plan is a Guaranteed Endowment Plan – i.e. the plan provides lumpsum benefits on policy maturity. This plan has both Single Pay and Limited Pay options with flexibility to choose the policy term upto 35 years. This plan creates a cushion of guarantee for your savings by providing both protection benefits across policy term to help safeguard all your dreams.
2. What are the significant features of ABSLI Assured Savings Plan? Key features of ABSLI Assured Savings Plan are:
Fully guaranteed benefits.
Loyalty Additions post premium payment term to boost your maturity corpus, provided all due premiums have been paid.
Higher maturity benefit for higher premium paying policies.
V
Comprehensive risk cover in case of death of life insured.
V
Cover for Spouse in the same policy through Joint Life Protection option.

V

Flexible Premium Payment Terms, Policy Terms and Sum Assured Multiples for customizing policy benefits.
Additional risk cover through Riders.

Plan Structure At inception you choose -
1. Premium - amount you need to pay during Premium Paying Term.
2. Premium Paying Term & Policy Term – number of years you wish to pay premium & number of years you wish to be covered.
3. Pay Mode – The way you wish to pay your premium (annual, semi-annual, quarterly, or monthly)
4. Joint Life Protection Option: Under this option, Customer (primary life insured) and their spouse (secondary life insured) are covered under the same policy.

The following details are about Assured Savings Plan (ASP)
 Joint Life Protection Option: Under this option, Customer (primary life insured) and their spouse (secondary life insured) are covered under the same policy.
5. Sum Assured Multiple - A multiple of annualized/single premium, i.e., amount you commit to pay in a policy year and your age at policy inception.
6. Protection Riders – for Increased protection.
4. What is the Entry Age of Life Insured acceptable under this plan? The minimum age at entry for Single Life is 30 days and for Joint Life is 18 Years. The maximum age at entry is 65 Years for Single Life, 50 Years for Joint Life and Single Pay Option B.
5. What is the Maturity Age of Life Insured acceptable under this plan? The minimum maturity age is 18 Years. The maximum maturity age is 85 Years and 70 Years for Single Pay Option B.

12. Can the c ustomer increase or reduce his premium amount? No, Customer cannot increase or reduce his premium amount during the policy tenure.

 Can the c ustomer increase or reduce his premium amount?

13. Can this plan also be taken on Joint life basis?
YES, this plan can be taken on single life as well as on joint life basis .
Under the Joint Life Protection option, two lives i.e. Customer (primary life insured) and their Spouse (secondary life insured) are covered under the same policy and have joint ownership of the policy . The sum assured applicable for the spouse shall be equal to 20% of applicable sum assured of primary life insured . Customer can opt for this option at the inception of the policy subject to the attained age of primary life insured & secondary life insure d is less than or equal to 50 years .
No rider s can be opted under this option and t his option cannot be discontinued except due to the unfortunate demise of either of the lives who are insured .

20. What are the benefits offered under this plan? The plan offers Loyalty Additions | Guaranteed Maturity Benefit | Death Benefits in addition to the facility of loans and policy surrender benefits. 21. What are the Loyalty Additions in this plan?

The following details are about Assured Savings Plan (ASP)

The plan offers Loyalty Additions | Guaranteed Maturity Benefit | Death Benefits in addition to the facility of loans and policy surrender benefits.
21. What are the Loyalty Additions in this plan?
Loyalty Additions will accrue under the policy as % of Total Premiums Paid at the end of each Policy year after the Premium Payment Term till policy Maturity (only if all due premiums are paid) . For Single Pay policies, Loyalty Additions will accrue from the end of the 2nd Policy Year. Accrued Loyalty Additions shall be payable in event of death of life insured or at policy maturity whichever is earlier.

In case of Joint Life Protection option, the Loyalty Additions shall accrue on the Sum assured applicable for the primary life insured and shall be payable on policy maturity.

In event the Customer survives t ill the end of the policy term, Maturity Benefit in the form of Lumpsum payout shall be payable.

23. What is the Guaranteed Maturity Benefit under this plan?
The lumpsum amount which is guaranteed to be paid as lumpsum on maturity of the policy, in accordance with the terms and conditions of the policy provided all due premiums have been paid is the Guaranteed Maturity Benefit under the policy. It is defined as a % of Total Premiums Payable over the Premium Payment Term, excluding the taxes, rider premiums, underwriting extra premiums and loadings for modal premiums, if any . The Guaranteed Maturity Benefit rate depends on the age of the Policyholder at the inception of the policy, Premium Paying Term and the Policy Term chosen, Joint Life Option Chosen and the

The following details are about Assured Savings Plan (ASP)
 The Guaranteed Maturity Benefit rate depends on the age of the Policyholder at the inception of the policy, Premium Paying Term and the Policy Term chosen, Joint Life Option Chosen and the Sum Assured Multiple chosen (if the Policyholder is above 50 years of age the time of Policy inception).
24. What is the Sum Assured on Death under this plan ?
The Sum Assured on Death is :
For Single Pay Policies:
- Sum Assured
For Limited Pay Policies : Higher of ,
- 150% of the Total Premiums Paid up to the date of death
- Sum Assured
where Sum Assured is equal to a multiple, depending upon Life Insured’s age at inception of the Policy, of the Annualized Premium or Single Premium.
25. What is the Death Benefit under the plan?
In the event of death of the Life Insured during the Policy Term and provided that the Policy is In -force, the Death Benefit will be payable to the Nominee. Nomination can only be effected if the P olicyholder and Life Insured are the same.
The Policyholder will receive a lumpsum guaranteed “Death Benefit” which shall be , if:
1) Death of the Life Insured happens any time up to the 25th Policy Year ,

The following details are about Assured Savings Plan (ASP) The Policyholder will receive a lumpsum guaranteed “Death Benefit” which shall be , if: 1) Death of the Life Insured happens any time up to the 25th Policy Year , - Sum Assured on Death; plus - Accrued Loyalty Additions (if any) 2) Death of the Life Insured happens any time after the 25th Policy Year , - Higher of Sum Assured on Death or Guaranteed Maturity Benefit; plus - Accrued Loyalty Additions (if any) 26. What is Staggered Death Benefit under the plan ? On the death of the life insured, the nominee and secondary life (on death of primary life in case of Joint Life Protection) , as the case may be, has an option to take the amount of Death Benefit in equal Annual/ Monthly instalments over a period of 5 years from the date of death of Life Insured, instead of a Lumpsum payout. Once the Instalment mode has been opted by the nominee at the time of benefit payment, it cannot be changed later on.

28. What is the Surrender Benefit under this plan?
The policy can be surrendered only after it has acquired a surrender value. For Singe Pay policies, Surrender Value is available at any time after policy issuance. For Limited Pay policies, a Surrender Value is available upon receipt of all premiums due in the first two policy years. The Policyholder can surrender the policy any time before the end of Policy Term. The surrender value is the higher of the Guaranteed Surrender Value (GSV) and the Special Surrender Value (SSV).

The following details are about Assured Savings Plan (ASP)
 The surrender value is the higher of the Guaranteed Surrender Value (GSV) and the Special Surrender Value (SSV).
The Guaranteed Surrender Valu e (GSV) shall be:
- GSV Factor 1 * Total Premiums Paid; plus
- GSV Factor 2 * Accrued Loyalty Additions (if any) GSV Factor 1 (% of Total Premiums Paid)
The policy will also be eligible for a Special Surrender Value. This Special Surrender Value is not guaranteed and can be reviewed by ABSLI from time to time. The Surrender Value calculation has been built in the illustration system for your benefit . The policy shall be terminated once the Surrender Value is paid.
29. Is loan facility available under this plan?
Yes. Customer can take a loan against the policy once it acquires a surrender value.
➢ The minimum loan amount is Rs. 5,000 and the maximum is 8 0% of the then applicable Surrender Value less any outstanding Policy loan balance . On exercising this option, the Policy shall automatically get assigned to the Company to the extent of the outstanding Policy loan balance.

The following details are about Assured Savings Plan (ASP)
 On exercising this option, the Policy shall automatically get assigned to the Company to the extent of the outstanding Policy loan balance.
➢ We shall charge interest on the outstanding loan balance at a rate declared by us f rom time to time based on then prevailing market conditions.
➢ The death benefit, maturity benefit or surrender benefit will be reduced by the outstanding loan balance (if any).
➢ If the outstanding policy loan plus Loan interest exceed s the surrender value , the company will inform the Policyholder of the same with a 90 -days advance notice to repay such outstanding Loan balance along with applicable interest.
30. Can customer avail of tax benefits under this plan?
You are advised to consult your tax advisor for details.
31. What are the additional benefits applicable under this plan ?
Yes, enhanced guaranteed maturity benefit (4% enhanced GMB) will be provided for e mployees of Aditya Birla Group companies, Agents of Aditya Birla Sun Life Insurance and Employees of Corporate Agents and Brokers, buying their policies directly from ABSLI .

The following details are about Assured Savings Plan (ASP)
 will be provided for e mployees of Aditya Birla Group companies, Agents of Aditya Birla Sun Life Insurance and Employees of Corporate Agents and Brokers, buying their policies directly from ABSLI . No commission is payable on these policies.
Section E
Premium Discontinuance and Revival
32. What happens if Customer does not pay premium s on time?
If Customer is unable to pay premium by the due date, he will be given a grace period of 30 days (15 days in case of monthly mode frequency) and during this grace period all coverage under your policy will continue.
33. What happens if Customer does not pay premium s within the grace period?
If Customer does not pay the premium s within the grace period, the following will be applicable: If less than two full years premiums have been paid then policy will lapse and all benefits will cease immediately. Otherwise, the policy will continue on a Reduced Paid -Up basis.
In case of death during the grace period, ABSLI will recover the unpaid premium due from the death benefit payable.
34. What is a Reduced Paid -up policy ?
If Customer discontinues paying premiums, after having paid premiums for at least 2 full policy years , policy will no t

The following details are about Assured Savings Plan (ASP)

If Customer discontinues paying premiums, after having paid premiums for at least 2 full policy years , policy will no t lapse but continue on a Reduced Paid -Up (RPU) basis. Under Reduced Paid -Up;
➢ The RPU Sum Assured, RPU Sum Assured on Death and RPU Guaranteed Maturity Benefit shall be equal to the Sum Assured, Sum Assured on Death and Guaranteed Maturity Benefit respectively multiplied by the RPU Factor. Where, “RPU Factor” means the ratio of the number of Instalment Premium paid on date, over the total number of Instalment Pr emium originally due for the Policy Term.
➢ The Policy will not be entitled for any Loyalty Additions once it has been converted into Reduced Paid -Up Policy . :selected: :selected: :selected: :selected: ➢ Rider benefit, if any, will cease.
35. What is the Death Benefit for a Reduced Paid -up policy ?
In the event of Customer ’s death any time up to the 25th policy year, the RPU Death Benefit will be:
- RPU Sum Assured on Death,In the event of Customer ’s death any time after the 25th policy year, the RPU Death Benefit shall be:
- Higher of RPU Sum Assured on Death or RPU

The following details are about Assured Savings Plan (ASP)

In the event of Customer ’s death any time up to the 25th policy year, the RPU Death Benefit will be:
- RPU Sum Assured on Death,In the event of Customer ’s death any time after the 25th policy year, the RPU Death Benefit shall be:
- Higher of RPU Sum Assured on Death or RPU Guaranteed Maturity Benefit
The RPU Death Benefit shall be subject to the minimum of 150% of Total Premiums Paid up to the date of death of the Life Insured.
In case Joint Life Protection Option is taken, the death benefit applicab le in case of death of Primary Life Insured is RPU Sum Assured on Death (subject to minimum 150% of Total Premiums Paid) and the death benefit applicable in case of death of Secondary Life Insured is 20% of the RPU Sum Assured.
36. What is the Maturity Benefi t for a Reduced Paid -up policy?
On the Customer ’s survival until the end of the Policy Term, the RPU Maturity Benefit i.e. the RPU Guaranteed Maturity Benefit shall be payable as a lump sum and the Policy shall immediately and automatically terminate. In case Joint Life Protection Option is taken, the RPU Guaranteed Maturity Benefit shall be payable on Maturity Date irrespectiveive of survival of the Life Insured/s.

The following details are about Assured Savings Plan (ASP)
 In case Joint Life Protection Option is taken, the RPU Guaranteed Maturity Benefit shall be payable on Maturity Date irrespectiveive of survival of the Life Insured/s.
37. How can Customer revive his policy?
Customer can revive policy for its full coverage within five years from the due date of the first unpaid premium by paying all outstanding premiums together with interest and/or late fees as declared by us from time to time and by providing evidence of insurability satisfactory to us.
Policy Termination
38. When will this policy terminate?
The policy shall immediately and automatically terminate on the earliest occurrence of any of the following events:
For a single life policy
➢ date of payment of free look cancellation amount; or
➢ the date of payment of Surrender Value; or
➢ the date of settlement of Death Benefit; or
➢ the date of payment of Maturity Benefit; or
➢ the date on which the Revival Period ends after Your Policy has lapsed as per Premium Discontinuance provision
For a joint life policy
➢ date of payment of free look cancellation amount; or
➢ the date of payment of Surrender Value; or
➢ the date of payment of Maturity Benefit; or

For a joint life policy

➢ the date of payment of Maturity Benefit; or
➢ the date on which the Revival Period ends after Your Policy has lapsed as per Premium Discontinuance provision
On termination of this Policy, all other benefits, including Riders attached to the Policy will immediately and automatically terminate.
Operational Guidelines
39. What application form is required for ABSLI Assured Savings Plan?
The “Common Application Form MAJOR ” & “Common Application Form MINOR ” would be used for this plan. In case of Joint Life Protection Option, the “ Joint Life Addendum Form ” will also have to be used. 40. Can this plan be taken by NRIs? Yes, this plan can be taken by NRI customers.
41. Can life insured & proposer be different under this policy?
Yes, life insured and proposer can be different under this policy for Single Life option. But if Joint Life is opted, the proposer and the Primary life Assured needs to be the same person.
42. Can the policy be sourced through Online and Direct Marketing Channel?

The following details are about Assured Savings Plan (ASP)
 But if Joint Life is opted, the proposer and the Primary life Assured needs to be the same person.
42. Can the policy be sourced through Online and Direct Marketing Channel? If yes, what are the additional benefits the policyholder will get while sourcing policies through these platforms?
Yes, the policy can be sourced through Online and Direct Marketing channels. For policies sold through Online and Direct Marketing channel, the GMB is enhanced by 4% for Limited Pay policies and by 2% for Single Pay policies.
43. What are the additional benefit s the policyholder will get if he/she is an Employee of Aditya Birla Group Companies, Agent of ABSLI or Employee of Corporate Agents and Brokers?
For Employees of Aditya Birla Group companies, Agents of Aditya Birla Sun Life Insurance and Employees of Corporate Agents and Brokers, the GMB is enhanced by 4% for Limited Pay policies and by 2% for Single Pay policies, if they buy their policies directl y from ABSLI. No commission is payable on these policies.
Example: If an employee of ABSLI aged 35 years buys a policy directly from ABSLI, with PPT 6 years and PT 25 years, the GMB of 1 66% of Total Premiums Payable

The following details are about Assured Savings Plan (ASP)

Example: If an employee of ABSLI aged 35 years buys a policy directly from ABSLI, with PPT 6 years and PT 25 years, the GMB of 1 66% of Total Premiums Payable is enhanced by 4% = 1 72.64 % (1 66% *1.04).
44. What are the other channels through which this product can be sourced?
This product can be additionally sourced through Insurance Marketing Firms, Referrals, Distance Marketing , Agency Channel , POS, Brokers, Corporate Agents, Direct Marketing and Web Aggregators .
45. Is the plan allowed under Employer -employee, HUF, Partnership, Keyman, MWP?
The plan can be issued under Employer -employee , MWP and HUF . It cannot be issued under Partnership and Keyman Insurance .
46. What is the GST rate applicable for the policy ?
For Year 1, the GST rate applicable is 4.5% and from the 2nd year onwards, the GST rate applicable for the policy is
2.25%.
47. Can policyholder get online discount & employee discount simultaneously?
No, the policyholder can avail any one discount at any given time while sourcing the policy.
48. Will the policy auto vest on completing 18 years for minor lives?

The following details are about Assured Savings Plan (ASP)

No, the policyholder can avail any one discount at any given time while sourcing the policy.
48. Will the policy auto vest on completing 18 years for minor lives?
Yes, when the policy is issued on a minor life, the policy will vest in favor of Life Insured after attainment of majority of the Life Insured.
49. How is the plan different than GMS?
The plan is different than GMS in the sense that this product provides better competitive IRRs as compared to GMS as well as most of it’s competitors. It has a simple product design, where the customers pay for a certain period of time and receive lum psum corpus at maturity . It also provides the customers, the flexibility to choose PPT/PT combinations, where the running Policy Term and Premium Payment Te rm can be utilized to create customized proposition for the customers as per their needs.
50. Will GMS be available along with ASP?
No, GMS will not be available with ASP. GMS will be withdrawn just before the launch of ASP.
51. What is the target customer segment for this product?
Customers between the ages of 25 -50 who are looking to generate guaranteed lumpsum benefits to achieve their various

The following details are about Assured Savings Plan (ASP)

Customers between the ages of 25 -50 who are looking to generate guaranteed lumpsum benefits to achieve their various life milestones are the target customers for this product. From Young people planning to travel to their dream destination, to parents planning for their child’s marriage and to people investing for their retirement, ABSLI AssuredSavings is the one -stop solution for every lumpsum money requirements.
52. What is the freelook period applicable for this product?
The Policyholder will have a free look period of 15 days from the date of receipt of the policy document and period of 30 days in case of electronic policie s and policies obtained through distance mode, to review the terms and conditions of the policy and where he/sh e disagree to any of those terms or conditions, he/she has the option to return the original policy document to us for cancellation, stating in writing the reasons for his/her objection, he/she shall be entitled to a refund of the pre mium paid subject to a deduction of a proportionate risk premium for the period of cover and the expenses incurred by us on medical
